children.heading: Access to coverage in territories
children.label: a Access to coverage in territories
children.text: The Secretary may waive such requirements of this part, including , insofar as the Secretary determines it is necessary to secure access to qualified prescription drug coverage for part D eligible individuals residing in a State (other than the 50 States and the District of Columbia).section 1395w–103(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Application of demonstration authority
children.label: b Application of demonstration authority
children.text: The provisions of section 402 of the Social Security Amendments of 1967 () shall apply with respect to this part and part C in the same manner it applies with respect to parts A and B, except that any reference with respect to a Trust Fund in relation to an experiment or demonstration project relating to prescription drug coverage under this part shall be deemed a reference to the Medicare Prescription Drug Account within the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund.Public Law 90–248

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of an individual described in subparagraphs (A) through (D) of who as of the last day of a calendar quarter in 2010 has incurred costs for covered part D drugs so that the individual has exceeded the initial coverage limit under for 2010, the Secretary shall provide for payment from the Medicare Prescription Drug Account of $250 to the individual by not later than the 15th day of the third month following the end of such quarter.section 1395w–114a(g)(1) of this titlesection 1395w–102(b)(3) of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: The Secretary shall provide only 1 payment under this subsection with respect to any individual.

children.heading: Treatment of certain complaints for purposes of quality or performance assessment
children.label: d Treatment of certain complaints for purposes of quality or performance assessment
children.text: In conducting a quality or performance assessment of a PDP sponsor, the Secretary shall develop or utilize existing screening methods for reviewing and considering complaints that are received from enrollees in a prescription drug plan offered by such PDP sponsor and that are complaints regarding the lack of access by the individual to prescription drugs due to a drug management program for at-risk beneficiaries.

children.label: A
children.text: trends in ingredient cost reimbursement, dispensing fees, incentive payments and other fees paid by PDP sponsors offering prescription drug plans and MA organizations offering MA–PD plans under this part to essential retail pharmacies (as defined in paragraph (2)) with respect to the dispensing of covered part D drugs, including a comparison of such trends between essential retail pharmacies and pharmacies that are not essential retail pharmacies;

children.label: B
children.text: trends in amounts paid to PDP sponsors offering prescription drug plans and MA organizations offering MA–PD plans under this part by essential retail pharmacies with respect to the dispensing of covered part D drugs, including a comparison of such trends between essential retail pharmacies and pharmacies that are not essential retail pharmacies;

children.label: C
children.text: trends in essential retail pharmacy participation in pharmacy networks and preferred pharmacy networks for prescription drug plans offered by PDP sponsors and MA–PD plans offered by MA organizations under this part, including a comparison of such trends between essential retail pharmacies and pharmacies that are not essential retail pharmacies;

children.label: D
children.text: trends in the number of essential retail pharmacies, including variation in such trends by geographic region or other factors;

children.label: E
children.text: a comparison of cost-sharing for covered part D drugs dispensed by essential retail pharmacies that are network pharmacies for prescription drug plans offered by PDP sponsors and MA–PD plans offered by MA organizations under this part and cost-sharing for covered part D drugs dispensed by other network pharmacies for such plans located in similar geographic areas that are not essential retail pharmacies;

children.label: F
children.text: a comparison of the volume of covered part D drugs dispensed by essential retail pharmacies that are network pharmacies for prescription drug plans offered by PDP sponsors and MA–PD plans offered by MA organizations under this part and such volume of dispensing by network pharmacies for such plans located in similar geographic areas that are not essential retail pharmacies, including information on any patterns or trends in such comparison specific to certain types of covered part D drugs, such as generic drugs or drugs specified as specialty drugs by a PDP sponsor under a prescription drug plan or an MA organization under an MA–PD plan; and

children.label: G
children.text: a comparison of the information described in subparagraphs (A) through (F) between essential retail pharmacies that are network pharmacies for prescription drug plans offered by PDP sponsors under this part and essential retail pharmacies that are network pharmacies for MA–PD plans offered by MA organizations under this part.

children.label: A
children.text: is not a pharmacy that is an affiliate as defined in paragraph (4); and

children.label: i
children.text: a rural area in which there is no other retail pharmacy within 10 miles, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: a suburban area in which there is no other retail pharmacy within 2 miles, as determined by the Secretary; or

children.label: iii
children.text: an urban area in which there is no other retail pharmacy within 1 mile, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Publication of list of essential retail pharmacies
children.label: A Publication of list of essential retail pharmacies
children.text: For each plan year (beginning with plan year 2028), the Secretary shall publish, on a publicly available internet website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, a list of retail pharmacies that meet the criteria described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (2) to be considered an essential retail pharmacy.

children.heading: Required submissions from PDP sponsors
children.label: B Required submissions from PDP sponsors
children.text: For each plan year (beginning with plan year 2028), each PDP sponsor offering a prescription drug plan and each MA organization offering an MA–PD plan shall submit to the Secretary, for the purposes of determining retail pharmacies that meet the criterion specified in subparagraph (A) of paragraph (2), a list of retail pharmacies that are affiliates of such sponsor or organization, or are affiliates of a pharmacy benefit manager acting on behalf of such sponsor or organization, at a time, and in a form and manner, specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Reporting by PDP sponsors and MA organizations
children.label: C Reporting by PDP sponsors and MA organizations
children.text: For each plan year beginning with plan year 2027, each PDP sponsor offering a prescription drug plan and each MA organization offering an MA–PD plan under this part shall submit to the Secretary information on incentive payments and other fees paid by such sponsor or organization to pharmacies, insofar as any such payments or fees are not otherwise reported, at a time, and in a form and manner, specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: D Implementation
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary may implement this paragraph by program instruction or otherwise.

children.heading: Nonapplication of Paperwork Reduction Act
children.label: E Nonapplication of Paperwork Reduction Act
children.text: Chapter 35 of title 44 shall not apply to the implementation of this paragraph.

children.heading: Definition of affiliate; pharmacy benefit manager
children.label: 4 Definition of affiliate; pharmacy benefit manager
children.text: In this subsection, the terms “affiliate” and “pharmacy benefit manager” have the meaning given those terms in .section 1395w–112(h)(7) of this title

children.heading: Biennial report on enforcement and oversight of pharmacy access requirements
children.label: f Biennial report on enforcement and oversight of pharmacy access requirements

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , and at least once every 2 years thereafter, the Secretary shall publish a report on enforcement and oversight actions and activities undertaken by the Secretary with respect to the requirements under .February 3, 2026section 1395w–104(b)(1) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: identifiable information about individuals or entities unless such information is otherwise publicly available; or

children.label: B
children.text: trade secrets with respect to any entities.
